mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue projections and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or signal fails to predict direction during recovery phases (accuracy < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.0 percent annually forces central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation triggered months, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending capacity and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which correlates positively with broader economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no directional bias (accuracy ~ 50%) during housing starts uptrends, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer prices and falling industrial production signals a supply shock regime that erodes corporate margins and triggers risk aversion, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 65% of supply-shock triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive power over single-series baselines.
